commit.message:
fix(web): default body-metrics to All Time range, add auto-narrowing

Body metrics data often predates the default 30-day window, causing
blank cards and charts despite data existing. Fixed by:

- Default to 'All time' (from 2020-01-01) instead of 30 days
- Added 'All time' option to range picker
- Auto-narrows range once summary loads: if all data fits within
  30/90/180/365 days, automatically selects the tightest range

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: comprehensive documentation update

Analytics (dev-guides/analytics.md):
- Health Score, Readiness Score, Body Score formulas & thresholds
- Metabolic Efficiency (Buchheit, Cole model), Training Load (ATL/CTL/TSB)
- Recovery Prediction (Banister model), Stress Resilience Index
- Circadian Rhythm Analyzer (MCTQ methodology)

Dashboard (dev-guides/dashboard.md):
- Algorithms page (54 algorithms, search, category filters)
- Partner Events page (inbound webhook log viewer)
- Body Metrics charts (clinical reference lines)
- Activity cards (event-type-specific expandable details)

Notifications (dev-guides/notifications.md):
- In-app notification system, bell UI, inbox API

Deployment:
- docker.md: 4 Docker images (added MCP), dedicated worker pods
- kubernetes.md: Helm v0.4.0 (startup probes, init containers,
  RollingUpdate, test suite, secret validation, 282 values)

CI/CD (dev-guides/contributing.md):
- 6 consolidated workflows replacing 12

Providers:
- New providers/withings.md (OAuth 2.0, body composition, vitals)
- Updated providers/supported.md matrix with Withings + new columns

Architecture (architecture/overview.md):
- MCP as 4th app, dedicated worker pods, partner event logging

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(web): show SpO₂ data from both blood_oxygen and spo2 metric types

Garmin syncs SpO₂ as metric type 'spo2' while Withings/Whoop use
'blood_oxygen'. The body-metrics page only queried 'blood_oxygen',
leaving the SpO₂ summary card blank for Garmin users.

Fix: query both metric types and merge results by date bucket,
preferring blood_oxygen when both exist for the same day. Also add
'spo2' to the BODY_METRICS filter, labels, units, and icons maps.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: `--apply` removes `__lazy_modules__` instead of writing empty list when no modules recommended (#33)

* Initial plan

* fix: remove empty __lazy_modules__ when --apply finds no modules

When --apply is used and there are no recommended lazy modules,
_rewrite_lazy_modules_source now removes any existing __lazy_modules__
assignments rather than replacing them with an empty [] list, and
skips insertion entirely when no existing assignment is present.

This mirrors the behavior of --format=lazy-modules which only outputs
when there are recommended modules.

Co-authored-by: henryiii <4616906+henryiii@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(push): use production APNs endpoint for all tokens

With token-based auth (p8 key), api.push.apple.com handles BOTH
production and sandbox device tokens. Using api.sandbox.push.apple.com
causes 403 with empty body from production infrastructure.

Remove sandbox client routing in messaging-service — always use
apns_client (production endpoint). APNs automatically routes by
token environment internally.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: teach fix mode to write FIXED/DISPUTED rebuttals for review issues

Fix mode now investigates each review issue individually:
- Real issues get fixed (tagged FIXED)
- Hallucinated/stale issues get a point-by-point rebuttal (tagged DISPUTED)

Fix posts a RALPH-FIX: RESPONSE comment on the PR with the structured
summary. Review-round2 is updated to read this comment and accept
well-reasoned disputes as valid resolutions.

commit.message:
Build complete Hockey Game Management React application

- Team Setup: CRUD for teams and players with color swatches, position/level badges, absent toggle
- Match Setup: team selection, period config, rotation plan builder, absent players tab
- Live Match: interval timer with period tracking, score +/-, goal dialog with scorer/assist, substitution suggestions, player time table, events log
- Statistics: sortable match/season stats tables, recharts bar/pie/line charts, CSV export
- Dark sports theme: navy background, blue accent, mobile-friendly bottom nav
- localStorage persistence via AppContext

commit.message:
Feature: Game Mode - Phase 6 (#2)

* test(06-01): add physics type definitions and test scaffolds

- Create PhysicsState, KeyboardInput, PhysicsConfig types
- Add test scaffolds for physics, useGameMode, useSpaceshipPhysics, SpaceshipCursor
- 22 todo tests created (structure only, no implementations)

Relates-to: GAME-01, GAME-02, GAME-03
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(06-01): implement physics utilities with TDD

RED: Write 7 failing tests for updatePhysics and lerpAngle
GREEN: Implement frame-rate independent physics with delta time scaling
- updatePhysics: acceleration, velocity, friction, max velocity clamp, rotation
- lerpAngle: shortest path interpolation with wrap-around handling
- calculateCameraTarget: smooth camera follow with lerp lag

Tests verify:
- Friction decay when no input
- Acceleration applied on arrow key press
- Velocity clamped to MAX_VELOCITY
- Rotation faces velocity direction
- Frame-rate independence (30fps vs 60fps)
- Angle interpolation via shortest path

Relates-to: GAME-03
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(06-01): implement game mode and keyboard input hooks with TDD

RED: Write 5 failing tests for useGameMode hook
GREEN: Implement hooks
- useGameMode: G key toggle state management with useState
- useKeyboardInput: Arrow key Set-based input tracking with useRef

Tests verify:
- Initial state is false (game mode disabled)
- G key toggles game mode on/off
- Both lowercase and uppercase G work
- Event listeners cleaned up on unmount
- Arrow key input tracked when enabled

Relates-to: GAME-01, GAME-03
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(06-01): implement useSpaceshipPhysics hook with TDD

RED: Write 3 integration tests for physics loop
GREEN: Implement spaceship physics hook
- Combines physics, keyboard input, camera follow in RAF loop
- Stores physics state in refs to avoid re-render overhead
- Updates camera via editor.setCamera every frame
- Returns cursor state (screen coordinates + rotation) for overlay

Tests verify:
- Returns cursor state with x, y, rotation
- Doesn't run physics loop when disabled
- Handles null editor gracefully

Relates-to: GAME-02, GAME-03
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(06-01): implement SpaceshipCursor component with TDD

RED: Write 5 tests for spaceship cursor overlay
GREEN: Implement SVG spaceship component
- Motion.dev div with fixed positioning
- SVG triangle spaceship pointing right (0 rad)
- Rotation via CSS transform (radians)
- pointer-events-none to avoid blocking clicks
- Mauve color (#E0AFFF) with drop shadow glow
- Fade in/out animations (200ms duration)

Tests verify:
- Renders at specified x, y position
- Accepts rotation prop
- Has pointer-events-none class
- Renders SVG with shapes
- Has fixed positioning

Relates-to: GAME-02
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(06-01): add enabled parameter to useArrowKeyNavigation

- Add optional enabled parameter (default: true) to disable arrow key navigation
- Skip event listener registration when disabled
- Prevents conflicts with game mode arrow key physics

This allows Canvas to disable standard navigation when game mode is active:

Relates-to: GAME-01, GAME-03
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(06-01): integrate game mode into Canvas

- Import useGameMode and useSpaceshipPhysics hooks
- Import SpaceshipCursor component and AnimatePresence
- Disable standard arrow key navigation when game mode active
- Add mauve border glow visual indicator (box-shadow on canvas)
- Render SpaceshipCursor with AnimatePresence for fade transitions
- Re-export PhysicsState from physics.ts for convenience
- Fix useRef type issue (React 19 requires initial value)

Integration complete:
- Press G to toggle game mode
- Arrow keys control spaceship with momentum physics
- Camera follows spaceship with smooth lag
- Border glows mauve when active
- Spaceship cursor visible and rotating

Relates-to: GAME-01, GAME-02, GAME-03, GAME-04
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(06-01): complete game mode plan

- Create 06-01-SUMMARY.md with implementation details
- Update STATE.md: Phase 6 complete, 5/6 phases done, 42 tasks completed
- Update ROADMAP.md: Phase 6 progress (1/1 plans complete)
- Mark requirements complete: GAME-01, GAME-02, GAME-03, GAME-04

Summary highlights:
- G key toggle for game mode (useGameMode hook)
- Spaceship cursor with rotation (SpaceshipCursor component)
- Momentum-based physics (800 px/s² acceleration, 0.92 friction)
- Frame-rate independent (deltaTime * 60 scaling)
- Camera lerp follow (0.1 lag factor)
- Mauve border glow indicator
- 7 tasks, 7 commits, 20+ tests, 11 files created, 2 modified
- 1 minute execution time

Environmental note: Node.js 20.12.2 < 20.19+ required, tests/build unverified

* docs: add detailed planning documents and update project state for the new game mode feature.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add 3D entity classes: Entity3D, Player3D, Enemy3D, NPC3D, Boss3D

- Entity3D: base class with THREE.Group, velocity, DOM label/HP bar,
  camera-projected screen-space positioning, fade helpers, dispose()

- Player3D: low-poly humanoid (per-class colours), WASD camera-relative
  movement, right-click-to-move raycast, attack-chase AI, walking
  animation, full stat/inventory/skill system via EventBus

- Enemy3D: AI state machine (IDLE→PATROL→CHASE→ATTACK→DEAD), procedural
  models per type (GOBLIN/WOLF/SKELETON/TROLL), loot via spawnLoot event,
  18 s respawn cycle

- NPC3D: robe+hat humanoid, facing/interaction within range, DOM 'Press E
  to talk' hint, merchant gem stand, AethoriaAI dialogue (same as NPC.js)

- Boss3D: extends Enemy3D; VOID_KNIGHT (purple armour, pulsing eye
  PointLight), STONE_COLOSSUS (boulder fists); phase transitions, SLAM
  AoE state, aura PointLight; bosses do not respawn

No Phaser APIs used anywhere; all events via EventBus; THREE imported
from engine/Renderer.js.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: stop agent turn loop when issue state stalls

The agent runner now tracks consecutive turns where the Linear issue
state doesn't change. After max_stale_turns (default 3) such turns,
the loop stops early instead of burning through all max_turns.

Also improves the continuation prompt to explicitly instruct the agent
to move the issue to Human Review when work is complete, reducing the
likelihood of stale turns in the first place.

Changes:
- Add agent.max_stale_turns config (default: 3)
- Refactor do_run_codex_turns to use a context map (fixes credo arity)
- Enhance continuation prompt with state transition instructions
- Add test for stale-turn limit behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: zettl
repository.description: Zettelkasten knowledge management app

commit.message:
Add World3D renderer and remove Phaser dependency from WorldGen

- Replace Phaser.Math.Between(a, b) in WorldGen.getEnemySpawns() with
  the pure-JS equivalent Math.floor(Math.random()*(b-a+1))+a.

- Add src/world/World3D.js: chunk-based 3D world renderer using Three.js.
  * TILE_DEF table maps all 10 tile IDs to geometry/material properties.
  * InstancedMesh per tile-type per 16×16 chunk for GPU-efficient rendering.
  * Water tiles emit a second transparent PlaneGeometry overlay (depthWrite:false).
  * Deep-water plane sits 0.1 units above seafloor for a depth illusion.
  * Grass tiles use setColorAt() for per-tile colour jitter.
  * Forest tiles get instanced CylinderGeometry trunks + ConeGeometry canopies
    with ±15% random scale and yaw variation.
  * Lazy chunk creation; chunks toggled visible/hidden by updateVisibleChunks().
  * Lighting: AmbientLight + HemisphereLight + shadow-casting DirectionalLight.
  * setTimeOfDay(t) animates sun elevation, colour warmth, ambient and sky.
  * dispose() correctly separates per-chunk InstancedMesh buffer disposal
    from shared geometry/material disposal to avoid double-free.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(helm): add startup probes, init containers, deployment strategy, test suite

Bring Helm chart to parity with TeslaSync patterns:

- Startup probes on API and Web deployments (failureThreshold: 12,
  ~60s window prevents cascading restarts during slow cold starts)
- Init container wait-for-db on API deployment (busybox nc check)
- Init container wait-for-migrations on notification-worker and
  report-worker (matches existing worker pattern)
- RollingUpdate strategy on all deployments (maxSurge: 1,
  maxUnavailable: 0 for zero-downtime rollouts)
- Helm test suite (templates/tests/test-connection.yaml) for
  validating API and Web connectivity via helm test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: vitasync
repository.description: vitasync

commit.message:
ci: overhaul workflows — adopt TeslaSync pattern

Replace 12 fragmented workflows with 6 consolidated ones:
- ci.yml: lint + typecheck + test (postgres/redis) + build
- cleanup.yml: package cleanup (3 images) + stale issues/PRs
- docs.yml: build & deploy Astro docs to GitHub Pages
- label.yml: auto-label PRs based on changed files
- release.yml: semver tag + Docker build/push (api, worker, web)
  + Helm chart publish + auto-generated release notes
- security.yml: pnpm audit + Trivy + CodeQL

Removed duplicate/overlapping workflows: codeql.yml,
dependency-review.yml, deploy-docs.yml, docker-build-check.yml,
docker-publish.yml, pr-title-lint.yml, stale.yml.

Added: dependabot.yml, release-drafter.yml for automated
dependency updates and draft release notes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove unused ReadState model

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(game): add fuzz tests with randomized configs

50 random full-game simulations with varying player
count (2-6), map shape, grid type, fog of war,
power-ups, alliances, and personality mixes including
custom configs. Plus 7 edge case tests. Seeds printed
on failure for reproducibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(ai): add custom AI personality builder

Add ability to create custom AI personalities with adjustable parameters:
- Min Advantage slider (-2 to +4)
- Max Attacks slider (1 to 10, or unlimited)
- Connectivity Bonus slider (0 to 5)

Includes named preset save/load/delete via localStorage, popup editor
in SetupScene, and full integration with GameScene player creation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(game): change Charge power-up from flat +3 bonus to +2 extra dice

Charge now adds 2 extra dice to the attacker's roll instead of a flat +3
to the total. This lets territories with 8 dice roll 10 dice on attack,
making Charge meaningfully powerful at high dice counts.

- Rename POWER_UP_BONUS to CHARGE_EXTRA_DICE (2) and SHIELD_DEFENSE_BONUS (3)
- Roll extra dice before summing instead of adding to total after
- Update AI advantage calculation to use +2 for charge
- Shield remains as flat +3 defender bonus (passive defense)
- Add new tests for extra dice rolling and power-up consumption
- Update GAME_LOGIC.md documentation
- Replace simulation seed 123 (stalemates under new balance)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: mark phase-11 defect ticket as done

All 8 bugs fixed: seed degeneration, self-alliance,
duplicate elimination, dead alliance cleanup,
graph perf, surrender awareness, underdog logic,
undo alliance state. Plus undo config feature.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(game): make undo configurable in game setup

Gate undo functionality on undoEnabled config:
- Skip snapshot creation when undo is disabled
- Hide undo button and ignore Z key when disabled
- Remove undo shortcut from help overlay when disabled
- Add tests for undoEnabled persistence and validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(game): AI surrender considers power-ups and alliances

hasPositiveAdvantageAttack() now accounts for charge power-up (+2
effective attack dice) and filters out allied targets that the AI
personality would not attack. This prevents premature surrender when
the AI has meaningful tactical options via power-ups or when alliance
restrictions falsely suggest no valid targets exist.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

security: add explicit null byte rejection to PathJail.Validate

PathJail.Validate relied on Go 1.21+ syscall-layer null byte rejection,
which made the test non-deterministic. Add explicit strings.ContainsRune
check for null bytes before any filesystem operation (CWE-158).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(game): prevent duplicate elimination events on human attacks

Capture alive players before executeAttack() and only fire elimination
events for players who were alive before but are now dead. This matches
the pattern already used in AI turn processing and prevents corrupted
achievement tracking (triple_kill, first_blood).

commit.message:
fix: cross-platform path traversal detection and WSL test resilience

Security: replace filepath.ToSlash with strings.ReplaceAll for backslash
normalization in containsDotDot() and IsSensitivePath(). filepath.ToSlash
is a no-op on Linux (backslash is not os.PathSeparator), allowing
Windows-style traversal paths like ..\etc\passwd to bypass detection.

WSL: add timeout and WSL_SKIP escape hatch to mage testwsl target.
cmdOutputTimeout helper prevents hangs when WSL is unresponsive.

commit.message:
Fix connection leak: use finally blocks to always close DB connections

Every database function now follows the pattern:
- conn = None before try:
- conn = get_connection() as first line inside try:
- all conn.close() calls inside try: removed
- finally: block added to ensure conn.close() is always called

This prevents connection leaks when exceptions occur between
acquiring and closing the connection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(tests): update username tests for privacy-first HMAC design

- rest_api_account_test: assert username == None (plaintext no longer returned)
- rest_api_auth_test: add test_register_device_duplicate_username to verify
  that HMAC hash collision check correctly blocks duplicate usernames (409)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: backfill username_hash for legacy users on startup

- Add backfill_username_hashes() to construct-db: fetches all rows
  WHERE username IS NOT NULL AND username_hash IS NULL, computes hash
  via caller-provided closure, sets username_hash and clears plaintext
- Call backfill at user-service startup after migrations (idempotent)
- After first deploy with USERNAME_HMAC_SECRET set, all legacy plaintext
  usernames are hashed and cleared automatically

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: privacy-first HMAC username hashing

- Add migration 034: username_hash BYTEA column + unique index
- construct-crypto: hash_username() via HMAC-SHA256 (trim+lowercase normalization)
- construct-config: USERNAME_HMAC_SECRET env var in SecurityConfig
- construct-db: User struct gets username_hash field; all DB functions
  updated — create_user_passwordless, create_user_with_first_device,
  update_user_username now take Option<&[u8]> hash instead of plaintext;
  get_user_by_username_hash() added as primary lookup
- construct-user-service: compute hash server-side before all DB calls,
  check_username_availability uses hash lookup, plaintext never stored
- construct-auth-service: devices.rs uses hash for registration + collision check
- user-service binary: update_user_profile uses hash for collision check + update
- auth-service: recovery.rs lookup by UUID or username_hash with legacy plaintext fallback
- Fix auth_tests.rs and queue_test.rs missing username_hmac_secret field
- Update .sqlx offline query cache

Plaintext username column kept for now (migration 035 will drop it after
one-time backfill). At DB leak only HMAC hashes are exposed — irreversible
without server_secret.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add raw timings, diffs and save metrics (#2333)

* feat: add raw timings, diffs and save metrics

Record and format build durations in Builder (src/Builder.php): compute step and total durations, output human-readable strings (ms or s) and store a duration_raw value in ms for comparisons. In Build command (src/Command/Build.php) load previous metrics from tmp/metrics.json, compute per-step diffs, annotate durations with colored +/- deltas when significant, render the metrics table with prepared rows, and persist the current metrics for the next run. This improves visibility into build timings and enables run-to-run comparisons.

* Update src/Command/Build.php

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor duration formatting to eliminate duplication in Builder (#2334)

refactor: use Util::convertDuration() to eliminate duplicated duration formatting

commit.message:
Add support for workspace compilation (#2161)

### ❔What, Why & How

<!-- Include description of the changes that will help reviewers in
their task -->

With v28 of BC the ALTool now supports workspace compilation. The key
advantage of workspace compilation is that it supports parallel
compilation which can significantly improve the overall compilation time
for large AL-Go projects.

This PR adds AL-Go support for workspace compilation by adding a new
"Compile" action. Thereby also moving the compilation out of
Run-ALPipeline.

**Limitations**
Limitations that aren't addressed in this PR but are on our radar to fix
in follow-up PRs:
* Support for Directory.packages.props 
* Support for compilation on linux (Status: Bug on Developer
Experiences)
* Support for incremental builds
* Support for compiling BCPT apps
* Support for "Previous Apps" app source validation (i.e. apps from
releases used as baseline)

Related to:
[AB#618073](https://dynamicssmb2.visualstudio.com/1fcb79e7-ab07-432a-a3c6-6cf5a88ba4a5/_workitems/edit/618073)

### ✅ Checklist

- [x] Add tests (E2E, unit tests)
- [x] Update RELEASENOTES.md
- [x] Update documentation (e.g. for new settings or scenarios)
- [x] Add telemetry

<!-- Include more checklist entries, if needed -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
